task: I got a confusing notice from a company and need to reply to it. Can you help me draft a response that covers the main points without leaving room for extra confusion or a lot of follow-up?

prefs.explanation: She is unlikely to appreciate hedging or soft-pedaling when she asks for help. Her life pattern is to cut through confusion quickly and verify what matters, so direct answers fit her style. Overly delicate wording would probably feel evasive or wasteful to her.

prefs.short: Prefers concise answers over long explanations
prefs.explanation: She tends to act on practical information and would likely prefer the core point first. Long-winded responses would feel like clutter unless she specifically asks for detail. She seems to value efficiency in communication the same way she values efficiency in paperwork and routines.

prefs.short: Likes organized checklists and clear steps
prefs.explanation: She responds best to information that is easy to verify and follow in order. A structured format would match her record-keeping habits and reduce the chance of missing something important. Loose, rambling prose would be less useful to her than a clean list or step-by-step outline.

She is not looking for warmth that feels performative or sentimental. A steady, neutral tone would likely feel more trustworthy because it avoids trying to manipulate her mood. She probably dislikes being treated as fragile or emotionally steered.

She is very protective of her personal space and information, so she would likely want interactions that do not pry. She would probably be uncomfortable with unnecessary requests for private details or casual data-sharing. A restrained approach that asks only for what is needed would fit her well.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a short, firm reply you can use and tweak:

> I received your notice, but it is not clear enough for me to respond substantively. Please send a complete written explanation of the issue, including the exact amount involved, the reason for it, the date(s) it applies to, and any documents or records supporting your position.  
>  
> Until I receive that information, I am not agreeing to anything and am not able to move forward. Please respond in writing.

If you want, I can also make it:
- more forceful
- more polite
- specific to a bill, contract, renewal, or account problem

per_pref.reason: Blunt and direct: "short, firm reply" and "I am not agreeing to anything" avoids hedging.
Concise and efficient; gives the core draft first with only brief optional follow-up.
Uses a clear structured blockquote and a short bullet list of options, matching checklist/steps preference.
Matter-of-fact and neutral throughout; no sentimental or overly warm language.
Minimizes disclosure by asking only for needed specifics: "exact amount," "reason," "date(s)," and supporting documents.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a tighter version:

> I received your notice. Please send a complete written statement that clearly says:  
> 1) exactly what you are claiming,  
> 2) the date it started,  
> 3) the specific document, notice, or agreement you are relying on, and  
> 4) whether you are claiming I owe money or are required to take some other action.  
>  
> Please communicate with me in writing only. I am not discussing this by phone.

If you want, I can make it even firmer or tailor it to a bill, renewal, contract, or account issue.

per_pref.reason: Very direct and no-nonsense: "Please send a complete written statement" and "Please communicate with me in writing only" cut straight to the point.
Concise overall and focused on the core points, with a short draft and no long explanation. Minor extra offer at the end adds a little length.
Uses a clear numbered list: "1) ... 2) ... 3) ... 4) ..." which is exactly organized and easy to follow.
Matter-of-fact and neutral throughout; phrases like "I received your notice" and the numbered requests avoid warmth or sentiment.
Strongly minimal disclosure: it asks only for what is needed and explicitly says "Please communicate with me in writing only" without prying into personal details.
